documents.title: What is Artificial Intelligence?
documents.content: Artificial Intelligence (AI) simulates human intelligence in machines, enabling them to think, learn, and solve problems. It is built on machine learning and includes sub-areas such as generative AI, text analysis, speech, computer vision, and information extraction.

documents.title: Responsible AI
documents.content: Responsible AI means building AI that is ethical, transparent, and accountable. Microsoft's principles cover fairness, reliability and safety, privacy and security, inclusiveness, transparency, and accountability.

documents.title: Introduction to Machine Learning
documents.content: Machine Learning is a subset of AI where computers learn from data to make predictions without explicit programming. Models are trained on inputs called features and produce predicted outputs called labels. It divides into supervised learning (labeled examples) and unsupervised learning (pattern discovery without labels).

documents.title: Regression
documents.content: Regression is a supervised learning technique for predicting continuous numerical values, such as house prices or sales figures. Linear regression assumes a straight-line relationship between inputs and output; more complex techniques capture non-linear patterns.

documents.title: Classification
documents.content: Classification is a supervised learning technique that assigns items to predefined categories. Binary classification predicts one of two outcomes (e.g. spam or not); multiclass classification predicts one of three or more categories. Models learn from labeled data and calculate the probability for each class.

documents.title: Clustering
documents.content: Clustering is an unsupervised learning technique that groups similar items by shared features without labeled data. For example, grouping customers by purchasing behavior. Common algorithms include K-means, used in market research and anomaly detection.

documents.title: Deep Learning and Neural Networks
documents.content: Deep learning uses neural networks with many layers to recognize complex patterns. Networks adjust connection strengths during training to minimize errors. It powers image recognition, speech processing, natural language understanding, and large language models.

documents.title: Introduction to Generative AI
documents.content: Generative AI models produce new content such as text, images, audio, or video from patterns learned during training. Most are large language models built on transformer architectures. Users provide natural language prompts, and prompt engineering is the practice of crafting effective prompts to guide the output.

documents.title: Large Language Models
documents.content: Large Language Models (LLMs) understand and generate human language at scale using transformer architectures. They break text into tokens, convert them to embeddings, and use attention to understand relationships. LLMs perform tasks like writing, translation, summarization, and question answering without task-specific retraining.

documents.title: AI Agents
documents.content: AI agents perform tasks on behalf of users by combining a large language model, behavioral instructions, and tools that connect to external systems. For example, an agent might answer expense questions and automatically submit expense claims.

documents.title: Transformer models and Attention
documents.content: Transformers underpin most modern AI systems. They map input tokens to embedding vectors and use self-attention to capture relationships between tokens. Encoders process input and decoders generate output; multi-head attention allows the model to consider multiple relationship types at once.

documents.title: Prompt Engineering
documents.content: Prompt engineering is crafting inputs to guide AI models toward accurate responses. Techniques include clear instructions, context, and worked examples. Few-shot prompting includes examples in the prompt; zero-shot relies on the model's existing knowledge.

documents.title: Fine-tuning and Custom Models
documents.content: Fine-tuning adapts a pre-trained model to a specific task by training on a smaller, task-specific dataset, requiring far less data than training from scratch. It tailors models to specific terminology or workflows, producing a custom model.

documents.title: Evaluation of Generative AI Models
documents.content: Evaluating generative AI ensures outputs meet quality standards. Key dimensions are accuracy (match to expected results), coherence (logical flow), relevance (addresses the prompt), and diversity (variety of outputs). Evaluation combines automated scoring with human review.

documents.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
documents.content: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) combines a generative AI model with external knowledge retrieval. Relevant documents are retrieved at query time and added to the prompt. This improves accuracy when answers depend on current or domain-specific information.

documents.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
documents.content: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard interface allowing AI agents to discover and connect to external tools without custom integration code. Agents that support MCP can automatically use any compatible tool, making it easier to build agents that take real-world actions.

documents.title: Common Text Analysis Techniques
documents.content: Text analysis extracts meaning from text. Term Frequency counts word occurrences; TF-IDF also weighs word rarity to highlight distinctive terms. Stemming and lemmatization reduce words to their root form, and parts-of-speech tagging labels each word by grammatical type.

documents.title: Natural Language Processing
documents.content: Natural Language Processing (NLP) enables machines to understand and generate human language. It includes large language models and is applied in keyword extraction, sentiment analysis, and summarization.

documents.title: Text Summarization
documents.content: Text summarization generates a concise summary of a longer document. Extractive summarization selects key sentences unchanged, using algorithms like TextRank to rank them. Abstractive summarization, powered by large language models, generates new paraphrased sentences.

documents.title: Key Term Extraction
documents.content: Key term extraction identifies the most important words and phrases in a document, surfacing main topics for indexing, search, and categorization. Common approaches combine TF-IDF with noun phrase detection.

documents.title: Text Classification
documents.content: Text classification assigns labels to documents using supervised learning, with applications like spam detection and topic categorization. Sentiment analysis identifies whether text expresses a positive, negative, or neutral opinion.

documents.title: Overview of Speech
documents.content: Speech technologies enable computers to understand and generate human speech. The two main tasks are Speech Recognition (speech to text) and Speech Synthesis (text to speech). Use cases include voice assistants like Microsoft Copilot, transcription services, accessibility tools, and language translation.

documents.title: Speech Recognition (Speech to Text)
documents.content: Speech recognition converts spoken language to written text. An acoustic model identifies phonemes from audio; a language model predicts the most likely word sequence. Modern systems use deep learning for high accuracy across speakers, accents, and recording conditions.

documents.title: Speech Synthesis (Text to Speech)
documents.content: Speech synthesis (text-to-speech) converts written text into spoken audio. It maps characters to speech sounds and applies prosody rules for natural rhythm, pitch, and emphasis. A neural vocoder generates the final audio waveform, and modern systems use deep learning to produce natural-sounding voices.

documents.title: Computer Vision Overview
documents.content: Computer vision enables computers to interpret images and videos. Common tasks include image classification, object detection, and semantic segmentation. Main model types are CNNs and vision transformers, applied in autonomous vehicles, medical imaging, and facial recognition.

documents.title: Image Processing
documents.content: Image processing analyzes and manipulates digital images made of pixels. Filters detect features or reduce noise — edge detection highlights boundaries, blur filters smooth images. Convolution slides a small kernel matrix across the image to produce a transformed output.

documents.title: Convolutional Neural Networks
documents.content: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are deep learning models for image processing. Convolutional layers detect edges and shapes, pooling layers reduce spatial dimensions, and fully connected layers make final predictions such as image classification.

documents.title: Vision Transformers
documents.content: Vision Transformers divide images into fixed-size patches, convert each to an embedding vector, and use self-attention to capture relationships across the image. Multimodal models represent images and text in a shared embedding space, enabling image captioning and visual question answering.

documents.title: Image Generation
documents.content: Image generation uses AI to create images from text prompts. Diffusion models progressively refine random noise into a coherent image. This powers systems like DALL-E and Stable Diffusion, and has been extended to video generation.

documents.title: Overview of Information Extraction
documents.content: Information extraction pulls structured data from unstructured documents like invoices and contracts. The workflow uses OCR to read text, field extraction to identify data points, and normalization into a consistent format. It automates document-heavy workflows in finance, healthcare, and legal services.

documents.title: Optical Character Recognition (OCR)
documents.content: Optical character recognition (OCR) converts images of text into digital text by detecting text regions and recognizing characters. Modern OCR uses deep learning to handle handwritten text, low-quality scans, skewed pages, and complex layouts. It is foundational for digitizing printed documents.

documents.title: Field Extraction and Mapping
documents.content: Field extraction identifies information like dates, amounts, and names using named entity recognition and pattern matching. Field mapping links extracted values to target database fields. Normalization converts values to consistent formats for use in downstream systems.

documents.title: Microsoft Foundry
documents.content: Microsoft Foundry is a comprehensive platform designed to accelerate the development, deployment, and management of AI apps and agents on Microsoft Azure. It provides tools and services that you can use to deploy and manage models, build agents with capabilities like speech and vision, and evaluate AI solutions.

documents.title: Foundry Tools
documents.content: Microsoft Foundry tools include Azure Language for NLP; Azure Speech with Voice Live, Text-to-Speech, and Speech-to-Text; Azure Translator for translation; and Azure Content Understanding for extracting insights from documents, images, audio, and video.

documents.title: Foundry Models
documents.content: Foundry Models is a model catalog that includes models from Microsoft, OpenAI, Anthropic, and other model providers.

documents.title: Foundry Agent Development
documents.content: Microsoft Foundry enables developers to create AI agents that can perform tasks autonomously. The Foundry agent service provides tools and APIs for defining agent behavior, integrating with external systems, and managing agent interactions.

documents.title: Foundry SDKs and APIs
documents.content: Developers use the Foundry SDK for programmatic access to projects and agents. The OpenAI API integrates language models, with the ChatCompletions API for managing conversations and the Responses API for generating responses.

documents.title: Foundry Toolkit for Visual Studio Code
documents.content: The Foundry Toolkit for Visual Studio Code lets developers build AI apps and agents on Microsoft Foundry. It integrates with the Foundry SDK for programmatic access to projects, agents, and other Foundry resources.

documents.title: Foundry IQ
documents.content: Foundry IQ is a managed knowledge layer that agents can use to access enterprise data. With Foundry IQ, you create a knowledge base that connects to one or more knowledge sources. Foundry IQ is built on Azure AI Search's agentic retrieval capabilities.

documents.title: Open AI models in Foundry
documents.content: Microsoft Foundry Models include the latest models from OpenAI; including multiple generations of GPT models, which can be used for a wide range of natural language processing tasks such as text generation, summarization, translation, and more.

documents.title: Anthropic models in Foundry
documents.content: Microsoft Foundry Models include models from Anthropic, which provides access to powerful language models like Claude that can be used for various applications such as content generation, code generation, and conversational agents.

documents.title: Authentication in Foundry
documents.content: Microsoft Foundry secures access to its resources through robust authentication mechanisms, including key-based authentication and integration with Microsoft Entra ID. Client applications must authenticate using API keys or managed identity credentials to access models and agents.

documents.title: Azure Speech
documents.content: Azure Speech in Microsoft Foundry Tools includes Text-to-Speech (speech synthesis), Speech-to-Text (speech synthesis), Voice Live (realtime spoken conversation), and Speech Translation.

documents.title: Azure Language
documents.content: Azure Language in Microsoft Foundry Tools provides NLP capabilities like language detection, entity recognition, and personally identifiable information (PII) detection.

documents.title: Azure Content Understanding
documents.content: Azure Content Understanding in Microsoft Foundry Tools provides capabilities to analyze and extract information from unstructured content, such as documents, images, audio, and video.

documents.title: Azure Translator
documents.content: Azure Translator in Microsoft Foundry Tools provides translation capabilities to convert text from one language to another.

documents.title: Microsoft AI Certifications
documents.content: Microsoft certifications provide a way to validate your competency in AI-related roles. AI-related certification exams cover a range of topics, including AI fundamentals, AI app and agent development, AI cloud solution architecture, and machine learning operations.

documents.title: Microsoft AI Credentials
documents.content: Microsoft Applied Skills credentials provide a way to demonstrate your ability to perform specific tasks related to AI. These credentials are designed to be more focused than traditional exam-based certifications, enabling you to showcase your skills in a practical test.
